item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Idk why, but the Foundation finds a way to allow both serious philosophical horror and quirky humor like this. The avian bureaucracy is well characterized and draws out long frustration. The ability to teleport is the type of bizarre thing that makes the interesting and difficult for the Foundation to handle. I really like the flamingo's insistence that Foundation agents don't have sufficient security clearances. Apparently there efforts have nothing on the flamingo's bureaucracy. I particularly enjoy the Foundation raid that all the Flamingos just teleport away. I can just feel the Foundation's fustruation.

The fact that the flamingos can teleport a Foundation agent away is lowkey scary to be honest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even if I did, you don't have the security clearance, and I'm not getting involved with Avian Resources again. Accidentally marked a document as correct once, ended up in a disciplinary hearing. A 30-bird jury had to unanimously decide the outcome. Whole thing ended in a stalemate. They found me culpable, but couldn't decide on an 80 or 81 day period of isolation. Took over 5 years start to finish….

This is hilarious. Reminds me of the whole bureaucracy thing from Futurama.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this skip, super funny and the punchline comes out of no where without feeling forced.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don’t let this dissuade you, there’s always more room for avian skips! This was my first ever attempt at writing humour, so it’s awesome to hear you found it entertaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had an idea for something similar to this buy with owls. I'm sad that I have to part with it but this is really entertaining. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Avian bureaucracy" - this was one of the very first things I wrote down when planning this skip, glad to see it's followed through to the finished product. Glad you enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sure thing, apologies for that. I’ll get it sorted today.

Edit: I've reduced the size of the files and localised them. Please let me know if they need to be reduced further!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I remember this! Glad to see it polished up and on the mainsite. +1 from me here.

(although, the images aren't localized to the page. Would you like help with that and maybe resizing them so they doesn't take as long to load on mobile? What you've got is in the megabyte counts, which can be a reader deterrent for people on their phones with limited data plans)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I spent my entire career in the Federal Civil Service of the United States of America. This SCP came close to inducing a flashback.

I regret that I have but one upvote for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Avian bureaucracy was not what I expected from an article about Keter flamingos, but consider me pleasantly surprised. I always love me a Keter that is just strange and super quirky, albeit difficult to contain. Well-earned upvote from me.

item.title: SCP-4075: Birds of a Feather
item.content:encoded:
Did you know that most flamingos are non-migratory, and that most are monogamous for their lifetime?

Thanks to Rounderhouse, Wazikamawata, and Zyn for the incredibly valuable feedback. Thanks also to Espinoza et al. for helping me plot flamingo colonies in Northwest Venezuela.

+ Image Credits
- Image Credits

Image in main text:
Flamingo Colony
Image in Addenda 4 composite of:
Guajira Peninsula
Foundation Logo
